revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''上毛野 稚子''' (かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳)は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]…」
revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子''' (かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳)は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[663年]]（[[天智天皇]]2年３月）に、 {{quotation|前将軍（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）上毛野君稚子（かみつけの の わかこ）、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）、中将軍（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）巨勢神前臣訳語（こせのかむせき の おみ をさ）・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]（みわ の きみ ねまろ）・後将軍（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]臣（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）、大宅臣鎌柄（おおやけ の おみ かまつか）遣（つかは）して、二万七千人（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）を率（ゐ）て、[[新羅]]（しらぎ）を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とある。続けて、6月に、稚子らが「新羅の沙鼻岐奴江（さびきぬえ）、二つの城（さし）を取る」とあり<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>、戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになり、定説が出ていない。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどうなったのかは、不明である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[朴市秦田来津]] *[[狭井檳榔]] *[[安曇比羅夫]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]] *[[白村江の戦い]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の わかこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|わかこ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: 壁掛成蛸は三考する葦である がページ「[[上毛野君稚子]]」を「[[上毛野稚子]]」に移動しました: カバネを入れて記事を作成しました。
revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子''' (かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳)は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[663年]]（[[天智天皇]]2年３月）に、 {{quotation|前将軍（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）上毛野君稚子（かみつけの の わかこ）、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）、中将軍（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）巨勢神前臣訳語（こせのかむせき の おみ をさ）・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]（みわ の きみ ねまろ）・後将軍（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]臣（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）、大宅臣鎌柄（おおやけ の おみ かまつか）遣（つかは）して、二万七千人（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）を率（ゐ）て、[[新羅]]（しらぎ）を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とある。続けて、6月に、稚子らが「新羅の沙鼻岐奴江（さびきぬえ）、二つの城（さし）を取る」とあり<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>、戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになり、定説が出ていない。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどうなったのかは、不明である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[朴市秦田来津]] *[[狭井檳榔]] *[[安曇比羅夫]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]] *[[白村江の戦い]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の わかこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|わかこ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子''' (かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳)は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別・右京皇別にも同様の記述があり、[[下毛野氏]]と同祖とある。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[663年]]（[[天智天皇]]2年３月）に、 {{quotation|前将軍（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）上毛野君稚子（かみつけの の わかこ）、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）、中将軍（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）巨勢神前臣訳語（こせのかむせき の おみ をさ）・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]（みわ の きみ ねまろ）・後将軍（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]臣（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）、大宅臣鎌柄（おおやけ の おみ かまつか）遣（つかは）して、二万七千人（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）を率（ゐ）て、[[新羅]]（しらぎ）を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とある。続けて、6月に、稚子らが「新羅の沙鼻岐奴江（さびきぬえ）、二つの城（さし）を取る」とあり<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>、戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになり、定説が出ていない。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどうなったのかは、不明である。
[[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[朴市秦田来津]] *[[狭井檳榔]] *[[安曇比羅夫]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]] *[[白村江の戦い]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の わかこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|わかこ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

とある。続けて、6月に、稚子らが「新羅の沙鼻岐奴江（さびきぬえ）、二つの城（さし）を取る」とあり<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>、戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになり、定説が出ていない。

revision.comment: 史料文ミス、本文修正。
revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子'''（かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別・右京皇別にも同様の記述があり、[[下毛野氏]]と同祖とある。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[663年]]（[[天智天皇]]2年３月）に、、 {{quotation|前将軍<small>（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）</small>上毛野君稚子<small>（かみつけの の わかこ）</small>、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]<small>（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）</small>、中将軍<small>（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）</small>巨勢神前臣訳語<small>（こせのかむさき の おみ をさ）</small>・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]<small>（みわ の きみ ねまろ）</small>・後将軍<small>（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）</small>[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]<small>（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）</small>、大宅臣鎌柄<small>（おほやけ の おみ かまつか）</small>を遣<small>（つかは）</small>して、二万七千人<small>（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）</small>を率<small>（ゐ）</small>て、[[新羅]]<small>（しらぎ）</small>を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とあるのが最初である。続けて6月に、稚子ら全将軍の軍団が「新羅の沙鼻岐奴江<small>（さびきぬえ）</small>、二つの城<small>（さし）</small>を取る」<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>という戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、「沙鼻・岐奴江」と「沙鼻岐・奴江」のどこで区切るのかが判明していない。現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになる。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどのような働きをしたのか、そしてどうなったのかは、不明である。
[[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年

revision.comment: カテゴリー追加・順番入替、巨勢神前訳語をリンクなど。
revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子'''（かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別・右京皇別にも同様の記述があり、[[下毛野氏]]と同祖とある。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[663年]]（[[天智天皇]]2年３月）に、 {{quotation|前将軍<small>（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）</small>上毛野君稚子<small>（かみつけの の わかこ）</small>、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]<small>（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）</small>、中将軍<small>（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）</small>[[巨勢神前訳語|巨勢神前臣訳語]]<small>（こせのかむさき の おみ をさ）</small>・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]<small>（みわ の きみ ねまろ）</small>・後将軍<small>（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）</small>[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]<small>（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）</small>、大宅臣鎌柄<small>（おほやけ の おみ かまつか）</small>を遣<small>（つかは）</small>して、二万七千人<small>（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）</small>を率<small>（ゐ）</small>て、[[新羅]]<small>（しらぎ）</small>を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とあるのが最初である。続けて6月に、稚子ら全将軍の軍団が {{quotation|新羅の沙鼻岐奴江<small>（さびきぬえ）</small>、二つの城<small>（さし）</small>を取る。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>|}} という戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、「沙鼻・岐奴江」と「沙鼻岐・奴江」のどこで区切るのかが判明していない。現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになる。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどのような働きをしたのか、そしてどうなったのかは、不明である。
[[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（一）・（二）・（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(上）・（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]

== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（一）・（二）・（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(上）・（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[扶余豊璋|余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]

revision.comment: [[Category:7世紀の軍人]]を追加 ([[WP:HotCat|HotCat]]使用)
revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子'''（かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭島王|彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>などが知られている。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別・右京皇別にも同様の記述があり、[[下毛野氏]]と同祖とある。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[天智天皇]]2年（[[663年]]）3月に、 {{quotation|前将軍<small>（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）</small>上毛野君稚子<small>（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）</small>、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]<small>（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）</small>、中将軍<small>（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）</small>[[巨勢神前訳語|巨勢神前臣訳語]]<small>（こせのかむさき の おみ をさ）</small>・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]<small>（みわ の きみ ねまろ）</small>・後将軍<small>（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）</small>[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]<small>（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）</small>、大宅臣鎌柄<small>（おほやけ の おみ かまつか）</small>を遣<small>（つかは）</small>して、二万七千人<small>（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）</small>を率<small>（ゐ）</small>て、[[新羅]]<small>（しらぎ）</small>を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とあるのが最初である。続けて6月に、稚子ら全将軍の軍団が {{quotation|新羅の沙鼻岐奴江<small>（さびきぬえ）</small>、二つの城<small>（さし）</small>を取る。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>|}} という戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、「沙鼻・岐奴江」と「沙鼻岐・奴江」のどこで区切るのかが判明していない。現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになる。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどのような働きをしたのか、そしてどうなったのかは、不明である。
天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（一）・（二）・（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(上）・（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[扶余豊璋|余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]

上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[天智天皇]]2年（[[663年]]）3月に、 {{quotation|前将軍<small>（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）</small>上毛野君稚子<small>（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）</small>、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]<small>（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）</small>、中将軍<small>（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）</small>[[巨勢神前訳語|巨勢神前臣訳語]]<small>（こせのかむさき の おみ をさ）</small>・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]<small>（みわ の きみ ねまろ）</small>・後将軍<small>（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）</small>[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]<small>（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）</small>、大宅臣鎌柄<small>（おほやけ の おみ かまつか）</small>を遣<small>（つかは）</small>して、二万七千人<small>（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）</small>を率<small>（ゐ）</small>て、[[新羅]]<small>（しらぎ）</small>を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とあるのが最初である。続けて6月に、稚子ら全将軍の軍団が {{quotation|新羅の沙鼻岐奴江<small>（さびきぬえ）</small>、二つの城<small>（さし）</small>を取る。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>|}} という戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、「沙鼻・岐奴江」と「沙鼻岐・奴江」のどこで区切るのかが判明していない。現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになる。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどのような働きをしたのか、そしてどうなったのかは、不明である。
天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（一）・（二）・（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(上）・（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[阿曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[扶余豊璋|余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋}}, middle generals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq-" to "[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--"
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋}}, middle generals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋}}, middle generals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋}}, middle generals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft|12=draft}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=draft|12=draft}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=draft|12=draft}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft|12=draft}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=draft|12=draft}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=draft|12=draft}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=draft|12=draft}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft|12=draft}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=draft|12=draft}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=draft|12=draft}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ (カバネ)|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft|12=draft}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=draft|12=draft}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=draft|12=draft}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|ja|尚州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚南道|12=draft|12=draft}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=draft|12=draft}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Park Seung-Jin|ja|朴市秦田来津|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=draft|12=draft}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|ja|扶余豊璋|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-Shin|ja|金庾信|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Liu Ren-Gui|ja|劉仁軌|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|ja|文武王|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''上毛野 稚子'''（かみつけの の わかこ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]。
== 経歴 == [[上毛野氏|上毛野君氏]]については、『[[日本書紀]]』巻第五にある、[[崇神天皇]]の長男の[[豊城入彦命]]の夢の話<ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年1月10日条</ref>や、巻第七にあるその孫の[[彦狭島王|彦狭嶋王]]の[[東山道]]派遣の話<ref name="#1">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月5日条</ref>、息子の[[御諸別王]]の東国経営の話<ref name="#1"/>などが知られている。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別・右京皇別にも同様の記述があり、[[下毛野氏]]と同祖とある。
上毛野稚子の名前が登場するのは、『書紀』巻第二十七によると、[[天智天皇]]2年（[[663年]]）3月に、 {{quotation|前将軍<small>（まへのいくさのきみ＝第一軍の将軍）</small>上毛野君稚子<small>（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）</small>、[[間人大蓋|間人連大蓋]]<small>（はしひと の むらじ おほふた）</small>、中将軍<small>（そひのいくさのきみ＝第二軍の将軍）</small>[[巨勢神前訳語|巨勢神前臣訳語]]<small>（こせのかむさき の おみ をさ）</small>・[[三輪根麻呂|三輪君根麻呂]]<small>（みわ の きみ ねまろ）</small>・後将軍<small>（しりへのいくさのきみ＝大三軍の将軍）</small>[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]<small>（あへのひけた の おみ ひらぶ）</small>、大宅臣鎌柄<small>（おほやけ の おみ かまつか）</small>を遣<small>（つかは）</small>して、二万七千人<small>（ふたよろづあまりななちたり）</small>を率<small>（ゐ）</small>て、[[新羅]]<small>（しらぎ）</small>を打たしむ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>}}
とあるのが最初である。続けて6月に、稚子ら全将軍の軍団が {{quotation|新羅の沙鼻岐奴江<small>（さびきぬえ）</small>、二つの城<small>（さし）</small>を取る。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年6月条</ref>|}} という戦果をあげたことが記されている。この「沙鼻岐奴江」であるが、「沙鼻・岐奴江」と「沙鼻岐・奴江」のどこで区切るのかが判明していない。現在の[[慶尚北道]][[尚州市]]にあたる「沙比」とすると、西から新羅に侵入したことになり、[[慶尚南道]]三嘉にあたる「三岐」とすると、南から侵入したことになる。
[[白村江]]での[[唐]]・新羅連合軍との決戦は、その2ヶ月後であった<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>。その際に、上毛野稚子がどのような働きをしたのか、そしてどうなったのかは、不明である。
天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月、[[八色の姓]]の制定により、上毛野君氏は一族の上毛野三千の労なども含め、長年朝廷に尽くしてきたことにより「[[朝臣]]」の[[カバネ|姓]]を賜与されている<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（一）・（二）・（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(上）・（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[阿曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いの戦死した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[扶余豊璋|余豊璋]] *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の わかこ}} [[Category:上毛野氏|わかこ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|qid=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|qid=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|qid=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|qq=|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|qid=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|qid=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|qid=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|qq=|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Library|qq=|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyasu Mō|qq=|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naoki Kōjirō|qq=|1=Kōjirō Naoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581787}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūōkōronsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央公論新社|1=Chuokoron-Shinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996095}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|qq=|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|qq=|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|qq=|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Library|qq=|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyasu Mō|qq=|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naoki Kōjirō|qq=|1=Kōjirō Naoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581787}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūōkōronsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央公論新社|1=Chuokoron-Shinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996095}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|qq=|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|qq=|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|qq=|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Library|qq=|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyasu Mō|qq=|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naoki Kōjirō|qq=|1=Kōjirō Naoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581787}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūōkōronsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央公論新社|1=Chuokoron-Shinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996095}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|qq=|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|qq=|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
