revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野稚子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|gōzoku|WD=Q84066}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|WD=Q55406951}}|father={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katana|WD=Q11359474}}|native_name=上毛野稚子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55406948]] Wikidata name: Kamitsukeno no Wakako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] of Japan. His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Library|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyasu Mō|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naoki Kōjirō|1=Kōjirō Naoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581787}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūōkōronsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央公論新社|1=Chuokoron-Shinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996095}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Park Seung-Jin]] – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Buyeo Pung|Buyeo Pung]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-Shin]] * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren-Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|Munmu of Silla]] * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}

His family name was [[:en:Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]].
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]], and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]]<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu]] and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern [[:en:Sangju|Sangju]], [[:en:North Gyeongsang Province|North Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:South Gyeongsang Province|South Gyeongsang Province]], the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki (Volumes 1, 2, 5)|publisher={{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}|year=1994-1995}} * {{cite book|title=The Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Volumes 1 & 2)|publisher={{ill|Kodansha Academic Library|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Library|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}|author={{ill|Ujiyasu Mō|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyasu Mō|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}|year=1988}} * {{cite book|title=The Formation of the Ancient Japanese State|author={{ill|Naoki Kōjirō|ja|直木孝次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naoki Kōjirō|1=Kōjirō Naoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581787}}|publisher={{ill|Chūōkōronsha|ja|中央公論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūōkōronsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中央公論新社|1=Chuokoron-Shinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996095}}|year=1965}} * {{cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans|editor={{ill|Saeki Ariyoshi|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015}}
== See also == * {{ill|Azumi Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Echi no Takutsu|en|Echi no Takutsu|WD=Q11521589|lt=Park Seung-Jin}} – A general who died in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Sai Binrō|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Binrō|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Luhara Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} – A general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} – A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Buyeo Pung|en|Buyeo Pung|WD=Q706581|lt=Buyeo Pung}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|en|Kim Yu-sin|WD=Q494501|lt=Kim Yu-Shin}} * {{ill|Liu Rengui|en|Liu Rengui|WD=Q701534|lt=Liu Ren-Gui}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|en|Munmu of Silla|WD=Q494532|lt=Munmu of Silla}} * {{ill|Churujō|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Churujō|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}}
