His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.
== Career == The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.
Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states: {{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}
By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory: {{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}
However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.
The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.
In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.

The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi}}.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|WD=Q39078946|lt=Kimi|1=Kimi (kabane)}}.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|1=Abe no Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|WD=Q42163|lt=Sangju|1=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province|1=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|WD=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province|1=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>

'''Kamitsukeno no Wakako''' (上毛野 稚子, birth and death years unknown) was a nobleman from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} of Japan. His family name was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|Kimi (kabane)|qid=Q39078946|lt=Kimi|1=Kimi (kabane)}}.

== Career ==
The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} is mentioned in several parts of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, including the story of {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, the eldest son of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Suijin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and his dream<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Suijin 48th year, 1st month, 10th day}}</ref>, as well as the story of his grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}} being dispatched along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|qid=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}}<ref name="#1">{{Nihon Shoki|Keikō 55th year, 2nd month, 5th day}}</ref> and his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} managing the eastern provinces<ref name="#1"/>. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also states that the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} were of the same lineage.

Wakako appears in the Nihon Shoki during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (663), in March, where it states:
{{quotation|The front general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|lt=Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子}}, {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|qq=}}, middlegenerals {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|qq=}}, and rear generals {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|qid=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hiketa no Omi Hirafu|1=Abe no Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja|大宅臣鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Omi Kamatsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were sent with 27,000 troops to attack {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 3rd month}}</ref>}}

By June of the same year, the generals' forces achieved victory:
{{quotation|They captured two fortresses at {{ill|Sabikinue|ja|沙鼻岐奴江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabikinue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 6th month}}</ref>}}

However, the exact location of "Sabikinue" is debated. If it corresponds to "Sabi" in modern {{ill|Sangju|en|Sangju|qid=Q42163|lt=Sangju|1=Sangju}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|en|North Gyeongsang Province|qid=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province|1=North Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion likely came from the west. Alternatively, if it corresponds to "Sangi" in {{ill|Sangju|ja|三嘉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|South Gyeongsang Province|en|South Gyeongsang Province|qid=Q41151|lt=South Gyeongsang Province|1=South Gyeongsang Province}}, the invasion may have originated from the south.

The decisive battle of {{ill|Baekgang|ja|白村江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekgang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白村江の戦い|1=Battle of Baekgang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q608727}} against the {{ill|Tang-Silla Alliance|ja|唐・新羅連合軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-Silla Alliance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} occurred two months later.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenji 2nd year, 8th month, 27th-28th days}}</ref> Wakako’s role in this final battle and his fate remain unknown.

In the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (684), the {{ill|Eight Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} were established, and the Kamitsukeno Clan received the title of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} in recognition of their longstanding contributions to the court, including the service of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|lt=Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Mikoto no Miko no Mitsumaro|qq=}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki|Tenmu 13th year, 11th month, 1st day}}</ref>
